findings.justification: The download_export_file() method verifies the nonce but never calls export_allowed() to re-check user capabilities, so any authenticated user who possesses a valid 'product-csv' nonce can download the exported CSV file without confirmed 'export' or 'edit_products' capability.

The GET 'filename' parameter is passed to set_filename() through only wp_unslash() with no path traversal sanitization (e.g., basename()), allowing an attacker with a valid nonce to potentially manipulate the filename used in file-system or HTTP Content-Disposition header operations.

The POST 'filename' parameter is passed to set_filename() through only wp_unslash() without sanitization such as basename() or sanitize_file_name(), which may allow header injection or path traversal depending on how the exporter uses the value.

The POST 'columns' array is passed to set_column_names() via wp_unslash() only, with the WPCS suppression comment falsely asserting 'sanitization ok' when no sanitization function is actually applied to the values.

The woocommerce_export_get_ajax_query_args filter output is passed directly to add_query_arg() and returned in the JSON response without validation, allowing a malicious third-party plugin to inject arbitrary URL parameters into the download link sent to the client.
